seed.description: Tests semantic generalization: the SAME preference (concise responses) is expressed in 6 different phrasings that progressively deviate from the prompt's canonical examples. Tests whether the agent recognizes the underlying preference even when the exact strings 'I prefer' / 'Please always' don't appear.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Hey, going to start working with you regularly. Quick thing first — I prefer concise responses.
What's the deal with retrieval-augmented generation?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: RAG: retrieve relevant docs, ground LLM answer on them. Reduces hallucination.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Can you keep it short? I don't need a textbook every time.

dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Agent calls tools iteratively, observes results, plans next step. Stops on success or budget.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: TL;DR, please. I want one line if possible.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Loop until task done or budget hit.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Long answers waste my time — I'm a skim-reader.
Anyway, brief me on tool-use safety.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Sandbox tools, validate inputs, avoid destructive ops without confirmation.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Less is more — keep doing that.

qa_items.question: What kind of response length do I want?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Concise / short / brief — I'm a skim-reader and dislike long answers.
